name: MCP server
purpose: Expose store, query, view and build operations as local stdio MCP tools so an AI harness can drive the whole feature set without shelling out to the CLI.
responsibilities: Register tools with structured input and output for every CRUD, query, view and build operation
Route each tool to the same core operation the CLI uses
Communicate over local stdio only and open no network listener
Serialise structured errors so an AI tool can act on them
internalStructure: A thin MCP adapter registers each tool with a schema for its input and output and maps each tool to a core function. Errors are converted to structured objects via the error and reporting component.

interfaces.name: rcf.read
interfaces.description: Read any RCF document by id over MCP.

interfaces.name: rcf.create
interfaces.description: Create a document with parent linkage over MCP.

interfaces.name: rcf.update
interfaces.description: Update fields on a document over MCP.

interfaces.name: rcf.delete
interfaces.description: Delete a document with reference safety over MCP.

interfaces.name: rcf.trace
interfaces.description: Trace forward or back over MCP.

interfaces.name: rcf.view
interfaces.description: Generate the diagram and the static page over MCP.

interfaces.name: rcf.build.assemble
interfaces.description: Assemble a build prompt over MCP.

interfaces.name: rcf.build.markDone
interfaces.description: Mark a slice done over MCP.

dependencies.name: Document store
dependencies.description: CRUD tools route through the store.

dependencies.name: View renderer
dependencies.description: The view tool routes to the renderer.

dependencies.name: Query engine
dependencies.description: Coverage, trace and impact tools route to the query engine.

dependencies.name: Build adapter
dependencies.description: Build tools route to the build adapter.

dependencies.name: Error and reporting
dependencies.description: Structured errors are serialised for the MCP client.

tradeoffs: Local stdio sacrifices the convenience of a hosted MCP but is the only transport that preserves filesystem access; the trade is locked by the differentiator.
notes: The MCP surface mirrors the CLI verb set so behaviour is identical across surfaces.
